title: The Kevin Jackson Show
author: Kevin Jackson
description: Kevin Jackson, the award-winning maestro of The Kevin Jackson Show, unleashes a daily two-hour masterclass in conservative commentary, political satire, and pop culture takedowns that hits like a Jason Bourne uppercut with Joe Rogan’s unfiltered swagger. Picture an unapologetic Black conservative voice—equal parts wit, intellect, and savage truth—delivering a red-pilled rollercoaster of humor and insight. As a best-selling author, TV host, political pundit, nationally celebrated speaker, and stand-up comedian, Jackson’s razor-sharp takes on politics, culture, and media echo the fearlessness of Rush Limbaugh, the cultural dissection of Ben Shapiro, the irreverence of Steven Crowder, and the conversational fire of Dan Bongino. His unique perspective highlights liberal hypocrisy with the precision of a sniper, who blends conservative values, libertarian edge, and a no-holds-barred comedic style that resonates with freedom-loving patriots. From dismantling woke nonsense to exposing Deep State shenanigans, Kevin Jackson’s show is a must-listen for fans of conservative talk radio, political podcasts, and truth bombs that leave the Left running for cover. Tune in for a high-octane mix of humor, wisdom, and unapologetic conservatism that would make Rush Limbaugh, Charlie Kirk, and Jesus all nod in approval.

title: The Upper Room Conversations
author: NewSong Ministries
description:
The Upper Room Conversations is a devotional and prayer podcast from NewSong Ministries in Benoni, South Africa.
Each episode offers a welcoming space to pause, listen to Scripture, reflect on God’s Word and pray. Through biblical teaching, pastoral encouragement and focused prayer, we seek to help listeners prepare their hearts for the presence of God and grow in their daily walk with Christ.
Be still. Listen. Reflect. Pray. Go.

title: More to the rainbow
author: Kori
description: This podcast, interviews people that identify as members of the LGBTQ so they can share more about their gender or sexuality with the world

title: VIVANT 悪役会議室
author: TBS RADIO
description:
「陸上自衛隊の裏の諜報部隊『別班』の諜報員、乃木憂助。彼の非情なる任務の前に、野望を砕かれ、敗れ去った者たちがいる。

乃木に自白剤を打たれ、偽装工作の果てに橋から吊るされた男……『テント』のモニター、山本巧。

国家の利権を貪り、隠しカメラの罠に堕ちて失脚した元バルカ外相……ワニズ。

親友への冷酷な裏切りを看破され、絶望の淵で悶絶した実業家……ゴビ。

すべてを失った『悪役』達に与えられた唯一の特権。 それは、自分たちを葬った乃木たちのその後、『VIVANT 第2 シーズン』の展開を監視し、底知れぬ謎を暴き、次なる復讐を企てること。

別班、公安に復讐するため、今宵もTBS ラジオの電波をジャックする… 日曜の夜は、終わらない――。

番組HP：https://www.tbsradio.jp/VIVANT-akuyaku

title: Backyard Sports Talk
author: CashMoney
description: We will mostly talk Wisconsin sports, but we will hit on other sports and teams. Recorded in the backyard of a Milwaukee suburb and hosted by Cash Money.

title: Behind the Inbox
author: Gorgias
description:
Your insider's guide to customer experience that actually converts.
Behind the Inbox is the podcast where the best ecommerce and CX leaders open up their real playbooks: the actual dashboards, tickets, and metrics behind world-class customer experience. Presented by Gorgias, each episode features Directors of CX, Heads of Support, and DTC founders on how they run customer support, roll out AI agents, cut response times, and turn customer service into a growth and retention engine. 
New episodes every other Tuesday. Subscribe for tactical, steal-this CX strategy.

title: Origin Archives
author: Evie Lucas
description:
Estimates exist of 60,000+ donor conceived Australians & majority still don’t even know that they’re donor conceived.
Origin Archives are resources for donor conceived Australians & a record of donor conceived people searching for their genetic identity, truth & transparency in an ever expanding for profit industry.
Origin Archives is a place to keep a record of our lives, uncover our histories, tell our stories & unfold our futures.

title: Studiō One Podcast
author: Oliabo Official
description:
Welcome to the Oliabo Studio Podcast, where Mediterranean living, timeless wellness, and modern storytelling come together.

Each episode explores the rituals, people, places, and ideas behind a more intentional way of life — from the olive groves of Italy to the everyday habits that help us feel more connected, nourished, and inspired. Through conversations on food, culture, longevity, creativity, and wellness, Oliabo Studio invites you to slow down, live beautifully, and rediscover the simple rituals that have shaped generations.

This is more than a podcast about olive oil. It’s a journey into the art of living well.

title: Beyond the Bells
author: Ariel, Tiffany, Hannah
description: Every school bell marks the end of a lesson, but perhaps the beginning of another story. Beyond the Bells is a place where conversations wander beyond classrooms and timetables, into the moments that shape who we become. From IB life and studying abroad to books, friendships, and growing up, this podcast is an invitation to step beyond the bells, where there's always another story waiting to be told.

title: The Big Picture
author: 10TV WBNS
description: 10TV’s The Big Picture is our commitment to keeping you informed and giving you a deeper understanding of the day’s headlines and what’s going on in your community.

title: It's Swell With Two People
author: Coco Jumbo and Vybe
description:
The show where we talk about anything, anyone and everything in between.
A weekly podcast that explores any topic, no matter how big, small, tall, short, known or unkown. Hosted by Australian drag legends Coco Jumbo and Vybe, stars of Rupaul’s Drag Race Down Under and Drag Race Down Under Vs The World.
Produced by Giuseppe Santamaria Studio

title: Futurecast by AI NEXTGEN America
author: Career Communications Group
description:
Futurecast by AI NEXTGEN America brings listeners into the conversations shaping what comes next.
Each episode explores the fast-moving world of artificial intelligence, quantum computing, machine learning, and emerging technologies through the voices of the experts building, studying, and applying them. From industry innovation and workforce transformation to practical upskilling, career readiness, ethics, and real-world use cases, Futurecast makes complex technologies accessible, relevant, and actionable.
Designed for professionals, students, leaders, educators, and lifelong learners, Futurecast helps audiences understand how intelligent technologies are changing the future of work and what skills will be needed to keep pace. Tune in for expert insight, forward-looking discussions, and practical guidance on how to adapt, grow, and prepare for the opportunities ahead.

title: Foxboro Unlimited
author: NBC Sports Boston
description: An aggregated “super feed” of NBC Sports Boston’s New England Patriots football podcasts: “Tom Curran’s Patriots Talk”, “The Next Pats Podcast”, and “The Quick Snap with Brian Hoyer and David Andrews”

title: Uncle Bobs Club Podcast
author: UBC Director - Richard Heron
description: A look into a charity with origins in 1941 raising funds for children's health that is still going strong today.

title: Guided to Christ
author: Ben Smith
description:
Welcome to the Guided to Christ podcast. You walk by people every day that have been guided to Christ and you have no idea. The purpose of this podcast is for you to hear their stories.  Jesus Christ is active in every single person's life. Each episode, you will hear stories of how He changed the trajectory of these people’s lives. 
Similarly, each episode is an invitation for you to look back at your own life to recognize your own moment in which you've been guided to Christ.

title: The Partners' Table: Conversations with Elite Leaders
author: Zynergy
description: Pull up a chair at The Partners' Table, where bold executives, entrepreneurs, visionary builders, and disruptive innovators share the stories behind their breakthroughs. Hosted by Zynergy's AI Lab, the innovation and venture incubator of Zynergy, this podcast explores the intersection of creativity, capital, and cutting-edge technology.Each episode dives into authentic conversations with founders and leaders shaping the future—across AI, fintech, marketplaces, and beyond. From startup challenges and inflection points to leadership lessons and what drives purposeful innovation, The Partners' Table reveals what it really takes to build something that lasts.Whether you're an investor, a founder, or just curious about the minds behind tomorrow’s biggest ideas—your seat is waiting.

title: The Etheric Double: Mapping the Energy Blueprint, with Elliot and Nadia
author: Pillar 5 Media
description:
What if the physical body you see in the mirror is only the densest layer of who you actually are? Welcome to The Etheric Double, a deep-dive podcast exploration into the invisible, energetic blueprint of human existence.
Based on Arthur E. Powell's foundational text, this series delivers a rigorous, structural breakdown of the human health aura and etheric anatomy. We analyze the precise movement of Prana, the mechanics of the force-centres, and the unseen physics governing vitality. This is a definitive study of the framework shaping human life.

title: When Nobody's Watching
author: The Cyber Escape Room Co.
description: Honest, tension-led conversations about what people really do once the security training's finished and nobody's looking. From The Cyber Escape Room Co., hosted by Amy Stokes-Waters. New episodes every fortnight.

title: Theo Von Clips
author: fivani8951@lasttea.com
description: Welcome to Theo Von Clips — your source for the best moments from Theo Von.Enjoy funny stories, unforgettable conversations, insightful moments, and the most entertaining highlights, all in one place. Whether you're a longtime fan or just discovering Theo's unique style, you'll find clips that are easy to watch and share.New clips uploaded regularly.

title: Josh Kɛ Shamo’s Corner
author: Joshua Afari and Manasseh Shamo Abbey-Cole
description:
Welcome to Josh Kɛ Shamo’s Corner 
Hosted by best friends Josh and Shamo, this podcast delivers engaging discussions on football, trending topics, personal growth, relationships, faith, entertainment, and the everyday experiences that shape our lives. From tactical football debates and thought-provoking conversations, funny moments, and honest opinions, there’s always something worth listening to.
Whether you’re a passionate football fan or someone who enjoys authentic conversations with a touch of humor, Josh Kɛ Shamo’s Corner is your place.
Where Sports Meet Motivation. 🎙️⚽🔥

title: WFMComFeedback.website | Whole Foods
author: Zaxby listen
description: Learn how the Whole Foods customer survey works with simple guides, FAQs, and participation tips. Visit https://wfmcomfeedback.website/ for complete survey information.

title: God Attachment Healing
author: Sam
description: Hi everyone! Welcome to the God Attachment Healing Podcast. I'm your host, Sam Landa. This podcast is dedicated to Christians who want to understand why they relate to God in the way they do. I explore how our early childhood relationship with our parents--specifically with how they met or did not meet our needs--influences how we relate to ourselves, the church, and to God. Because much of the pains and struggles of life are intertwined in these three areas, I discuss with my guests how we can find healing from the pain, confusion, doubt, and anger experienced in these relationships. If you're interested in learning more about your attachment style and how to heal from the pain you’ve experienced in the relationships mentioned above, then this podcast is for you. Welcome to the show! I'm happy you're here!

title: Million Dollar Problems
author: Luke Lloyd & Chris Hjelm
description:
Making money is hard. Keeping it, growing it, and knowing what to do with it can be even harder.

Welcome to Million Dollar Problems, a podcast about the financial challenges that come with success. Hosted by Luke Lloyd, founder of Lloyd Financial Group, and Chris Hjelm, Portfolio Manager for Connetic Ventures, the show explores the real-world decisions behind wealth, investing, taxes, retirement, business, family, and legacy.


Because having more money doesn't eliminate problems—it often creates more complicated ones.

How do you know when you have enough? How do you turn wealth into freedom? How do you protect what you've built? How do you avoid giving too much to the IRS? How do you pass wealth to the next generation without destroying their motivation? And perhaps the biggest problem of all: what do you do when money is no longer the primary thing driving you?


Million Dollar Problems brings real conversations, sharp insights, and practical financial planning strategies to the challenges that come with building and maintaining significant wealth.

Because when you have million-dollar problems, you need more than million-dollar solutions—you need a plan.

title: We've See This Movie Before: The Disruption of SaaS
author: Cosmo Mariano
description: In 2002, disrupting SaaS meant beating a $2M CRM check with a $65/month browser app. In 2026, AI is doing the exact same thing to SaaS itself. Cosmo Mariano sits down with three channel and venture veterans — Rod Baptie, Nick Tidd, and Jason Dea — to break down why the AI shift is following the same script as the SaaS shift 20 years ago: the objections, the economics, the bubble talk, and the businesses already getting disrupted along the way. No hype, no AI theater — just real operators talking through what's actually happening

title: Behind the Brush | The Painter and Decorator Podcast
author: Behind The Brush | The Painter and Decorator Podcast
description:
Behind the Brush | The Painter and Decorator Podcast
Behind the Brush is where painters, decorators and finishing professionals come together for real conversations about the people behind the trade.
Hosted by Gerard Donaghy from Paintworks NI Academy, each episode goes beyond paint and tools to explore the journeys, struggles, successes and lessons of some of the industry’s most respected professionals.
From business growth and pricing to mental health, family life, social media, craftsmanship and leadership, nothing is off limits. Whether you’re just starting out or have decades of experience, every guest has a story worth hearing and something valuable to share.
New episodes feature honest, unfiltered conversations with decorators, business owners, manufacturers and industry leaders from across Ireland, the UK and beyond.
If you’re passionate about painting and decorating, want to improve your business, and enjoy hearing the real stories behind the people shaping our industry, you’re in the right place.
Real. Raw. Relatable. Welcome to Behind the Brush.

title: The Faithful Agrarian Podcast

description: The Faithful Agrarian explores a wide variety of topics at the intersection of faith and agrarianism. It sits on the 3-legged stool of Creation Care - Agrarian Ministry - Churchland Stewardship.

Covering mostly Biology and Chemistry topics, Science will explore various topics with different elements to make each episode more interesting and different from one another like myth busters, interviews, story telling, humor, dramatic sound effects, etc.

Hosted by Dr. Ben Crites and Dr. Jon DeClerck, each episode explores the latest research, practical management strategies, and industry trends that help commercial cattle producers, seedstock breeders, and beef industry professionals make more profitable genetic decisions.

author: John Bradburn
description: A series of dramatic monologues. One night in Britain. The secrets, the desires, the hopes and the dreams of a disparate set of characters on one long summer night. Written & Directed by John Bradburn

title: Initial Insights - Weekly Bible Chat
author: Greg Allen-Pickett
description:
Initial Insights is a weekly conversation from Lake Grove Presbyterian Church. Pastors, staff, and guests gather for an informal discussion of the Bible passage that will be preached and taught the following Sunday.
Together, we share first impressions, ask questions, explore the context of the passage, and listen for what God may be saying through Scripture. These conversations help us begin shaping our sermons and invite our church family to start reflecting on the text before we gather for worship.
Come open the Word with us and discover your own initial insights for the week ahead.

title: The She Shines Podcast | Mindset, Nutrition, Lifestyle
author: Leah Van Dolder
description: Are you a busy working woman or female entrepreneur who's got a good handle on everything in your life except your weight and health? Join Leah Van Dolder, Weight Loss Dietitian, Mindset Coach, and Entrepreneur as she inspires her listeners to reclaim their health and achieve lasting weight loss with holistic, sustainable, and practical solutions. Leah helps her listeners ditch the all-or-nothing mentality towards getting healthy and empowers you to re-write your thought process so you can take consistent actions and be the healthiest & happiest version of you. More info available here: www.leahvandolder.com

title: Referable.biz
author: Paul Stephenson
description:
Join hosts Paul Stephenson and Graeme Crosbie to learn how to grow your service business through referrals. This podcast is aimed at small business owners, marketers and sales manager who want a practical guide to setting up and managing a referral network that is designed to generate a continuous stream of qualified leads and clients. The topics discussed are based on the book Referable.biz, also by Paul Stephenson, published in 2026.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

description: Providing bold political commentary, engaging debates, and honest conversations about Canada, the United States, and the issues shaping both countries. We dig deeper into political misconceptions, challenge misinformation, and separate strong evidence from empty talking points to help you cut through the chaos.

title: Wildthang Outdoors
author: Wildthang Outdoors

title: Pride Stories Daily
author: Pride Stories

Daily English audio stories adapted from original Pride Stories editorial articles.

Hosted by Elizabeth Hudson, join education leaders and practitioners from across Wellspring and beyond as we talk about life in the classroom, what’s going on in schools and how we can improve education for everybody.   Subscribe for free to access a new episode every fortnight and occasional bonus content too. Search for The Wellspring Way on Apple podcasts, Spotify or wherever you get your podcasts.

Each episode moves slowly through global stories with calm narration, vivid context, and a bedtime pace for listeners who want history while they fall asleep.

title: American Couple Files from Reddit | Boring History for Sleep Podcast

description:
Real couples. Real drama. Zero resolution.
Every episode, we dig into the messiest American marriage and relationship stories from Reddit — told slow, calm, and dry enough to put you to sleep. Whether it's a decade of silent resentment or a blowup over something stupid, we read it all like it's a history lecture nobody asked for.
Perfect for falling asleep, zoning out, or just feeling better about your own relationship.
New stories every week. Hit subscribe and sleep well. American Couple Files from Reddit

Each episode is paced for sleep with tense atmosphere, quiet dread, and unsettling details.

title: Beyond the Bell
author: Beyond the Bell
description: Welcome to Beyond the Bell the podcast where we go beyond the classroom and engage in conversations that matter most. Join us as we explore real challenges, share practical strategies, celebrate successes, and discuss innovative ideas that help schools thrive!

title: 10 Halloween Safety Tips for Pets
author: Jesse McDaniel
description: Halloween is a fun and spooky time of the year, but it can also pose some dangers for your furry friends. In this podcast, we will talk about some of the common hazards that pets may encounter during this festive season, and how to keep them safe and happy. Also, Jesse is an active blogger on CanadaPetCare, where she often shares content about pet care.

title: Dark Finds Podcast
author: Gary Colton
description: Some stories just hit different. Welcome to The Dark Finds Podcast, hosted by writer, true crime creator, and psychology enthusiast Gary Colton. Each episode takes you deep into the world of those who’ve lived through — or created — something extraordinary. From fighters, musicians, and war veterans to cult survivors, ex-cons, psychologists, and entrepreneurs, we explore the lives of people who exist on the edge of the norm. Through honest, often raw conversations, we uncover their journeys, insights, struggles, and what drives them.At its core, Dark Finds is fueled by one obsession: understanding human behavior in all its forms.

title: Second Order Podcast
author: Nate Dinet
description:
Second Order is a podcast for technology founders, COOs, and operations leaders who want to understand what actually happens between closing a deal and delivering on it. 
Hosted by Nate Dinet of The Step Manager, each episode features honest conversations with CEOs, heads of business operations, and senior executives at SaaS companies, AI-native startups, and tech services firms. Covering delivery chaos at scale, the founder bottleneck, bespoke traps that erode margins, and when to invest in technology versus people — if the gap between selling and delivering is where things get messy, Second Order is where you'll find the frameworks and hard-won insight to close it.

title: Thoughts I Probably Shouldn't Say Out Loud - Unfiltered | Unscripted
author: Maddie
description:
Thoughts I Probably Shouldn't Say Out Loud is exactly what it sounds like. Unfiltered, raw thoughts from a single mom navigating a corporate layoff 3 years ago, currently driving for $20/hr to pay the rent (and that's about it). No script, no rules, no topic off-limits. Just say it out loud. 
New episodes at least weekly or whenever a thought hits hard enough to hit record.

title: Route 66: America's Greatest Road Trip
author: Vanessa Woodgate
description:
In this eight-part series, journalist and broadcaster Sean Fletcher connects with his American roots, joined by famous figures who’ll help him pay tribute to the iconic Route 66 in its 100th year. Along the way he’ll learn how their lives were touched by it and meet the local characters keeping the spirit of this legendary highway alive.


From a lifetime spent up and down on a tour bus to life-changing adventures on two wheels, they all share their memorable stories, favourite stops and travel tips for anyone dreaming of taking the greatest road trip of them all.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: 2 Cents with Tash
author: Tash Marconi
description:
My 2 cents on all things mindset, strategy and the tools to help you bet on yourself again & again. If you are ready to BLAZE your own trail, DEFY the odds and become the LEADING figure of your life then this is your clubhouse. Lets BLAZE BABY!! 
Your host is Tash Marconi - a professional dancer of 17 years spanning work in film, tv, musical theatre, Artists and more as well as building her own business working alongside creative artists.

author: Izabelle and Sierra

title: Wyatt's Library
author: Wyatt M. Forbes
description: What started out as a blog on substack is also now a podcast. Enjoy my discussions, reviews, and analysis of books. Welcome to Wyatt's Library.

title: Against The Grain
author: Ben
description:
Most business content is built to be safe and shareable, not to be true. It sands off the edges, ignores incentives, and pretends that hype and headlines are strategy. The founders and operators who actually change things don’t follow that playbook. They learn to read the grain of their market, and then cut against it where it matters.


Against the Grain is a podcast for those people and the ones who want to join them.


Hosted by Benjamin Douglas Ray, a founder and growth strategist who lives at the intersection of capital, technology, and consumer behavior, the show uses a historical futurist lens to make sense of what’s coming next. Ben draws from Rome, Greece, Mayan trade networks, Chinese dynasties, Middle Eastern hubs like Dubai, and modern AI‑driven markets to show how the same underlying laws keep repeating:

  • Flows beat snapshots.
  • Incentives quietly shape everything.
  • Information advantage compounds.
  • Narratives steer capital.
  • Systems outlast heroes.


Each episode challenges a piece of modern groupthink -- fundraising myths, dashboard obsessions, “best practices” that don’t survive contact with reality -- and replaces it with timeless principles and practical frameworks.


The focus isn’t on specific projects or guru stories. It’s on how to design systems where human dreams and effort have a real shot at becoming outcomes.


If “not yet” is still your fuel, if you’d rather build something durable than chase the latest algorithm, and if you’re willing to think differently without losing the room, Against the Grain is your weekly reset.

title: Empire Against Empire.
author: Abby Histories
description: Empire Against Empire tells the stories of history's greatest empires, their rivalries, battles, and the events that shaped the world.

title: Impact Unscripted
author: Rahwa Mehari
description: Impact Unscripted showcases the many ways impact is made for the greater good, whether through a profitable company or a mission-driven nonprofit. Host Rahwa Mehari, Ed.D., sits down with founders, executives, and nonprofit leaders to uncover what first inspired them and how their organization plays a real role in creating change. These are conversations that move past the mission statement and into the strategy, purpose, and personal story behind the work. Because impact doesn't come in just one form, Impact Unscripted is where you hear all of them, one honest conversation at a time.

description: Chitkara University offers an online masters finance degree, online mba in digital marketing, and an online mba in pharmaceutical management.

title: Loft Boarding Hythe
author: L.W. Lofts
description: Loft boarding in Hythe is a smart, practical solution for homeowners who need more storage without the stress of major renovation work. It makes better use of existing space, improves home organisation, and can even contribute to better energy efficiency.

title: Disabled Parenthood Podcast
author: Erica Evans
description:
The Disabled Parenthood Podcast features conversations with disabled, chronically ill, and neurodivergent parents and parents-to-be exploring pregnancy, postpartum, parenthood, caregiving, identity, and the many ways people build families. 
My hope is that if you’re wondering what parenthood could look like for you, you’ll hear stories here that help you feel a little less alone.

title: Test podcast

description:
-------------------
*About the Podcast*
-------------------
Are you ready to transform your management style and drive real business performance? Welcome to your go-to guide for navigating the highs, lows and everything in between of corporate leadership.
Hosted by leadership expert *Jess Pressland* , this podcast cuts through the jargon to deliver honest stories, practical insights, and a candid look at the front lines of management.
In each episode, Jess shares the common pitfalls she regularly sees leaders make and provides you with the actionable strategies needed to get it right.
----------------------
*What You’ll Discover*
----------------------
* *The Good, the Bad, and the Missteps:* Candid stories of where leaders frequently go wrong and how to steer clear of the same mistakes.
* *Proven Management Frameworks:* Insights driven by Jess's proprietary methodology, the *JP Learning Loop*.
* *Actionable Advice:* Practical, real-world takeaways designed to elevate your leadership game, whether you are managing a small team or directing an entire organization.
--------------------------------
*Meet Your Host: Jess Pressland*
--------------------------------
As the founder and CEO of the *JP Group* , Jess has spent over 20 years developing managers at all levels—from first-time team leaders to executive-level suites.
Her impressive track record includes:
* *Decades of Hands-On Experience:* Rising through the ranks from trainer to Head of Training, Jess founded her own management development business in 2012.
* *Award-Winning Leadership:* Recipient of the prestigious *British Business Award for Leadership (2026)*.
* *Industry Recognition:* Featured on the cover of Elevate HR magazine for her transformative work in management development.
> 
> *Who is this for?*
> 
> Whether you are a newly promoted supervisor, a mid-level manager, or a
> seasoned C-suite executive, this podcast is designed to give you the
> tools, clarity, and confidence to lead effectively.

title: ISO-Standard Black Noise: The Complete 25-Track Series
author: Declan
description:
Welcome to the most technically rigorous ambient noise podcast available. This series presents 25 meticulously engineered Black Noise soundscapes, each designed to serve a specific therapeutic, cognitive, or restorative purpose.

What Is Black Noise?
Unlike White Noise (equal energy across all frequencies) or Pink Noise (balanced for human hearing), Black Noise occupies the deepest end of the acoustic spectrum. Mathematically defined as a 1/f^β signal where β > 2, Black Noise is characterized by overwhelming sub-bass energy, near-total high-frequency silence, and — in its purest form — vast expanses of technical silence punctuated by rare, high-amplitude transient spikes. The result is a profoundly dark, immersive, and physically felt listening experience that standard noise generators simply cannot reproduce.

Why This Series Is Different
Every episode in this series is synthesized from first principles using professional Digital Signal Processing (DSP) chains. We do not loop stock audio. Each track begins with a mathematically generated Brownian or spike-based noise floor and is then sculpted through cascaded Butterworth filters, ITU-R BS.1770 loudness normalization, true-peak limiting, and spatial processing — all calibrated to audiophile and ISO measurement standards.

The 25 Soundscapes Include:

• Sleep & Insomnia Relief: Velvety, Midnight, Tranquil, Serene, and Abyssal Black Noise — engineered with extreme low-pass filtering and zero transient spikes to maintain uninterrupted Delta-wave sleep cycles.

• ADHD Focus & Study Masking: Tectonic, Hypnotic, Granular, and Resonant Black Noise — utilizing rhythmic gating, sub-harmonic synthesis, and steady-state low-frequency drones to occupy the brain's distraction pathways without introducing cognitive fatigue.

• Tinnitus Masking & Auditory Therapy: Submerged, Veiled, and Umbra Black Noise — applying narrow band-pass filtering and heavy downward expansion to create a sonic "blanket" that gently obscures persistent high-frequency ringing.

• Deep Meditation & Somatic Grounding: Meditative, Ethereal, and Void Black Noise — incorporating 432Hz and 528Hz Solfeggio resonances, 4Hz Delta binaural beats, and anechoic spatial processing for profound stillness.

• Cinematic & Atmospheric Immersion: Cosmic, Lunar, Eclipse, Obsidian, Cavernous, and Infinite Black Noise — utilizing convolution reverb, Doppler modulation, and granular delay for expansive, otherworldly soundscapes ideal for creative work, reading, or sensory decompression.

Technical Specifications (Per Episode):
• Sample Rate: 48kHz (32-bit float internal processing)
• Loudness: -18 LUFS integrated (ITU-R BS.1770-4 compliant)
• True Peak Ceiling: -1.0 dBTP
• Filter Topology: Cascaded 4th and 8th-order Butterworth
• Non-linear Processing: 4x oversampled waveshaping (anti-aliased)
• Dithering: TPDF on 16-bit export

title: The Lazy Sage Podcast
author: Sankar Srinivasan
description:
The Lazy Sage Podcast

Ancient common sense for modern madness.

Life has become strangely complicated. We have apps to help us relax, videos that tell us how to sleep, and endless advice on becoming more productive. Yet many of us still feel rushed, distracted, and tired.

The Lazy Sage Podcast is a gentle escape from all that.

Each episode explores everyday topics like overthinking, stress, simple living, money, relationships, technology, work, sleep, kindness, and happiness through warm conversations, relatable stories, light satire, and timeless common sense.

There are no lectures, no life hacks, and no impossible promises. Just practical ideas that make modern life feel a little lighter.

Think of it as sitting on a park bench with the funniest uncle, the wisest grandmother, and a calm friend who has learned that most problems do not need bigger solutions—just simpler ones.

Whether you are walking the dog, making a cup of tea, driving home, or taking a break from your screen, these conversations are here to help you slow down, smile, and remember what really matters.

New evergreen episodes every week.

Put the phone down for a while.

Make yourself a cup of tea.

Take a slow walk if you can.

The world will still be there when you get back.

Until next time, this is The Lazy Sage.

- Sankar Srinivasan

Hosted by Ryan Stewart, each episode features honest conversations with people who have experienced the highs, losses, and consequences of gambling addiction, then found lasting recovery. From sports betting and casinos to online gambling and financial devastation, you'll hear real stories, expert insights, and practical tools for healing. Whether you're in recovery or supporting someone who is, hope starts here. New episodes every week as part of the **Sober as F*ck Podcast Ne

title: Country Chronicles
author: Christian De Martel
description: Country Chronicles takes you on a journey through the people, places and moments that shaped country music. Each episode explores a different chapter in the genre's rich history, bringing legendary artists, iconic songs and untold stories to life in a warm, cinematic documentary style. Whether you're a lifelong country fan or discovering the music for the first time, you'll uncover the history behind the hits and the voices that changed country forever. Because every great country song has a story.

title: Men's Varsity Letterman Jacket: The Stor
author: Jeric Kelvin
description:
The men's varsity letterman jacket is more than just a fashion piece. From its roots in American college culture to its place in modern streetwear, the varsity jacket represents achievement, confidence, teamwork, and individuality. This episode explores the history, design elements, and cultural importance of men's varsity jackets, including why this classic style continues to influence students, athletes, and fashion enthusiasts around the world.

Discover how wool bodies, leather sleeves, embroidered patches, and traditional varsity details transformed a simple jacket into a symbol of identity and personal expression.

Explore premium varsity jacket styles at DearVarsity: https://dearvarsity.com/

title: This Delicious Life™ - Self-Kindness, Less Servitude & Permission to Re-invent
author: Kim O'Hara
description:
If you are driving kids or managing the launch of adult children, fielding calls about aging parents' health, running a business or working over 40 hours, and don't seem to be able to find moments to self-honor and relish in the beauty of life, you get to re-boot with the little delicious moments. 
So many women 30-60 in the "sandwich generation" have become inundated with tasks and forgotten to do random acts of self-kindness. In This Delicious Life™ podcast, female guests from all professions share how they find these small spaces to bask in their self-love. Host Kim O'Hara also shares about her delicious life and the development of her mindset that is the genesis of her upcoming book, Live Your Delicious Life.

title: The Bespoken AI Show
author: Bespoken AI
description:
The Bespoken AI Podcast explores the technology, companies, and ideas shaping the future of Voice AI.
Hosted by John Kelvie and Diego Sandoval, each episode dives into the latest industry developments, real-world use cases, technical concepts, and the history behind voice assistants, conversational AI, contact centers, and AI agents.
We go beyond the headlines to explain why these technologies matter, what the industry is learning, and where Voice AI is headed next.

title: The AI w/ Scottie Podcast
author: TheAIwithScottieShow
description:
The AI with Scottie Podcast
We’re living in a tech-fueled paradise and sprinting toward a digital dystopia! Depending on the day, AI is either the ultimate tool freeing us up to create, or the overwhelming noise taking over everything.
The AI with Scottie Podcast straddles that exact line. We explore both sides of the coin: the incredible breakthroughs making life easier, and the cold reality of what happens when we automate the human touch. No hype, no confusing tech jargon—just honest conversations about what’s actually happening in the world of artificial intelligence.
Whether you’re looking to leverage the tech or trying to figure out how to keep your life and business grounded without it, you can trust this show to cut through the noise. Tune in for clear, unfiltered insights and the practical information you need to navigate a world that’s changing by the second.

title: Silent Thoughts: Mysterious Mind
author: Silent Thoughts
description:
Silent Thoughts is a calm, mysterious podcast about the hidden emotions of the human mind.
Each episode explores love, loneliness, fear, memory, dreams, attachment, overthinking, heartbreak, and the strange reasons people feel what they feel.
Through emotional storytelling and simple psychology, Silent Thoughts helps you understand the quiet thoughts we rarely say out loud.
This podcast uses AI-generated voice narration, with human editing and creative direction.
Welcome to Silent Thoughts: Mysterious Mind, The quiet mysteries of the human mind.

title: Voice To The Nations with Bosun Afolayan
author: Bosun Afolayan
description:
VOICE TO THE NATIONS is a broadcast platform ordained of God to raise an end-time band who like Gideon's army will break forth the pitcher of clay (subdue the flesh and it's works) so that the glorious light of God hidden in them can shine forth triumphantly to defeat and destroy the hordes of darkness that Satan has let loose on earth (JUDG. 7:16-22). The Bible says that we have this treasure in earthen vessels that the excellency of the power may be of God, and not of us" (2 COR. 4:7).

 

As powerful as light is, it's useless when it is covered, and for light to make great impact and serve more and more people, there is need for a raised platform. "Ye are the light of the world. A city that is set on an hill cannot be hid. Neither do men light a candle, and put it under a bushel, but on a candlestick; and it giveth light unto all that are in the house." -MATT. 5:14-15.

 

God has ordained this broadcast as a raised platform (candlestick) for sending forth the light of His word into all nations. As the light shines on you, the glory of God will manifest through you in an ever increasing dimension, and everything called darkness will bow. The LORD will move you from glory to glory in Jesus name. Amen.

title: Find Her Again
author: Amy Weaver and Ashley Ciminski
description: Join us as we intentionally create a space that helps you find the person that you may have lost for a bit. We will have guests from all walks of life, sharing their stories, as they lay out their defining moments that have crafted who they are today.

title: Making The Best Chance
author: wilson Mercy
description:
Every opportunity has the power to change your life—but only if you recognize it and act on it. Making The Best Chance is a podcast dedicated to helping you identify opportunities, overcome fear, make wise decisions, and turn everyday moments into meaningful breakthroughs.

Through inspiring stories, practical strategies, and thought-provoking conversations, each episode equips you with the mindset and confidence to maximize your potential in your career, relationships, business, finances, and personal growth. Whether you're standing at a crossroads, recovering from setbacks, or preparing for your next big move, this podcast will encourage you to stop waiting for the perfect moment and start making the most of the opportunities already in front of you.

title: Get Curious, Not Furious
author: Colette
description: Get Curious is a parenting podcast that helps you make sense of your child’s behaviour, emotions, and development. Each episode invites you to pause and look beneath the surface so you can understand what’s driving your child’s responses. With practical, relationship-focused insights, it supports you to respond differently—making everyday parenting feel easier, calmer, and more connected.

title: Case X Case
author: Deja Brady
description: I've always been drawn to true crime and the psychology behind it. I'm fascinated by what leads people to make unimaginable choices, how cases are solved, and the stories that often get lost behind the headlines. If you're as curious as I am about the minds of criminals, the twists of an investigation, and the details that make each case unique, grab a seat and let's dive into the story together.

title: The Growth Exchange Podcast
author: Dr. Tarence E. Lauchié
description:
Welcome to The Growth Exchange Podcast—where ideas take shape, conversations spark change, and insights inspire action.
 
Our podcast is dedicated to uncovering transformative ideas in leadership, personal growth, and innovation, with a focus on how faith, creativity, and entrepreneurship intersect to drive meaningful change.
 
Join us for thought-provoking discussions with innovators, leaders, and changemakers as we navigate the challenges and opportunities shaping today's world. Through inspiring stories of dedication and impact, we highlight how business, charity, and faith can work together to uplift lives and create lasting transformation.
 
From the role of arts and culture in sparking entrepreneurship to the power of creative partnerships, The Growth Exchange Podcast is more than just a platform—it's a space for meaningful dialogue, equipping you with the insight and inspiration to turn vision into impact.
 
Let's grow together.

title: NO EXCUSES
author: Fernando Mateo
description: No Excuses with Fernando Mateo is where New York’s biggest issues are debated without filters. Hosted by businessman and community leader Fernando Mateo, the show features candid conversations with elected officials, policymakers, journalists, and newsmakers on politics, public safety, immigration, education, business, and the stories shaping New York and America. No spin. No talking points. Just real conversations, accountability, and solutions.

author: Schneider Electric
description:
The Machine Room is a brand-new podcast series from Schneider Electric, exploring the challenges and trends facing the future of the global digital infrastructure industry. ​​
Led by Schneider Electric’s SVP and CMO for the Data Center business, Kevin Brown, hear senior leaders from key industry players for conversations that share insights into the heart of data centers and AI infrastructure - exploring the future of energy technology, and the power of digital innovation.

title: Who sent ya?
author: The Urban Center
description: In Chicago's politics, the old adage still fits — 'We don't want nobody, that nobody sent!' Who Sent Ya? is a podcast from The Urban Center bringing you the political insider perspective straight from the people shaping Chicago. Understand the why and the how we got here. Subscribe and tune in to weekly episodes. Who Sent Ya? Chicago, politics and more.

title: The Called to Build
author: Framed solutions
description:
The Called to Build is a podcast for Christian women who know they were created for more than simply earning a living; they were called to build.
Hosted by Dr Faith Liz, entrepreneur, coach, and Kingdom business mentor, this podcast equips women to discover their God-given calling, build profitable businesses, create Kingdom wealth, and steward their influence for God's glory.
Whether you're starting with an idea, a passion, or simply a desire to obey God's call, you'll learn how to build from little to much using biblical principles and practical business strategy.

title: Yahz Ink Live
author: Yahya Muhammad
description: Yahz Ink Live is a live music discovery platform where independent artists get the spotlight they deserve. Every episode features exclusive interviews, live performances, music premieres, freestyles, producer showcases, and real conversations with emerging talent. Whether you're an artist looking to be heard or a fan searching for the next big name, Yahz Ink Live is where music, creativity, and opportunity come together.

title: The enfolded and unfolding Light
author: Alaya
description:
The Enfolded and Unfolding Light is a contemplative podcast exploring spirituality, everyday life, and the deeper questions that shape the human experience. Through thoughtful discussions, personal reflections, and soul-stirring spiritual music, each episode creates space to pause, listen, and reconnect with the light within.

Whether reflecting on faith, purpose, healing, consciousness, relationships, or the mystery of existence, the podcast welcomes open-minded seekers from all backgrounds. It is a gentle invitation to discover what is already enfolded within us—and to embrace the light that continues to unfold through our lives.

title: PepperTree
author: Bryan Emmanuel
description: This is a space for fearless political commentary, sharp social critiques, and poetic call-outs that dig beneath the surface of current events, power dynamics, and societal realities. Hosted by filmmaker Bryan Emmanuel, we challenge mainstream narratives, break down complex political landscapes, and offer bold, uncompromised perspectives on the issues that shape our world today.

title: Live Longer. Live Stronger. Lessons from a Centenarian.
author: Brad Lowder & Keros Lowder
description: Live Longer. Live Stronger. is a personal, thoughtful podcast about how to age with strength, clarity end purpose. A father and son learn from longevity experts and the quiet lessons of someone who lived past 100. This is not a show about chasing youth, fads, or quick fixes. It’s an our building strength, resilience and meaning for the years ahead - through simple habits, community and purpose. If you’re thinking about the long game of life, this podcast is for you.

title: The Moving Archive
author: Ben Williams
description:
The Moving Archive is a media history podcast dedicated to uncovering the stories behind the moments that shaped the world of media.
From landmark broadcasts and groundbreaking films to influential television programmes, video games, podcasts, and cultural events, each episode explores not only what happened, but why it mattered and how its impact can still be felt today.
Created and hosted by Ben Williams, The Moving Archive brings together research, storytelling, and curiosity to help listeners discover the people, innovations, and turning points that changed media forever.

title: Triumph By Judy Wilson
author: Momentum Media Advertising
description: Embark on an epic, century-long journey through the dark secrets and soaring resilience of the West family as they battle multi-generational trauma, crime, and systemic injustice. Through the eyes of author Judy Wilson, you will witness a gripping descent from Depression-era Georgia to modern-day trials, transforming how you view family loyalty and survival. By the final episode, you will shift from an observer of tragedy to a champion of hope, discovering how to break free from ancestral chains.  Listeners will walk away with a profound framework for recognizing and breaking toxic generational patterns, learning to navigate the complex intersections of inherited trauma and mental illness. You will gain critical insights into the systemic biases of housing and justice systems, empowering you to better advocate for yourself and others. Finally, this season equips you with the emotional boundaries and resilience strategies needed to protect your peace while fostering healing within fractured relationships.  This podcast is custom-built for avid memoir readers, true-story lovers, and cycle-breakers who have fought to rebuild their lives from the ashes of a complicated family history. If you believe in the power of truth to conquer trauma, this season is your sanctuary and your battle cry. We made this for you because your own quiet resilience deserves to be seen, validated, and celebrated.

title: Fiber Optic Yarns: The Science and Magic of Color
author: Fiber Optic Yarns
description: A podcast for knitters, crocheters, spinners, weavers, and anyone fascinated by the beauty of color. Through practical demonstrations, inspiring projects, and explorations of the people, places, and stories that shape the colors we love, we'll discover why colors behave the way they do. Add a little science to the mix, and suddenly color is full of new possibilities, creativity sparkles, and every project becomes an invitation to explore.

description: A podcast hosted in the heartland of Wisconsin that highlights the real people behind the Midwest businesses Success. I will be sitting down with the entrepreneur that started it all and dissect what made them who they are today.

Join Beaux each week as he sits down with eyewitnesses to share real-life Bigfoot encounters and high-strangeness from across the Bible Belt. No fluff, no Hollywood staging—just honest people sharing extraordinary experiences they were never supposed to see.

Every episode is a real conversation with a founder, operator, investor or enabler about what building actually looks like — not the highlight reel. Because building is personal, and the story's always better than the press release.

title: Career Talk Podcast
author: Mikey D
description: Success doesn't happen by accident—it’s choreographed. Hosted by Great Day Radio’s DJ Mikey D, this podcast is your ultimate resource for career development in a rapidly changing world. Each week, we explore actionable advice on networking, personal branding, and professional growth. Combining the engaging storytelling of live radio with the practical tools of career coaching, DJ Mikey D helps you navigate your path to the top. Subscribe to Great Day Radio's Career Talk Podcast and start making every workday a Great Day!

title: The AI Agent Economy
author: Atin Agarwal
description:
The serialized audiobook of The AI Agent Economy: Why the Next Trillion-Dollar Industry Has No Employees — a new chapter every week, written and narrated by Atin Agarwal. From the invisible workforce already running businesses today to the dependency layer, the economics, the trust problem, and 15 dated, falsifiable predictions. A practitioner's field guide to the agent economy, for founders and operators who want the structure underneath the hype.
Narrated with an AI clone of the author's own voice. New chapter weekly. The complete book (ebook, paperback, hardcover) is on Amazon.

title: Backseat Legends
author: Nuface & GreatNight
description:
Backseat Legends is an Atlanta hip-hop history podcast. Host NuFace, the city's unofficial hip-hop historian, pulls up to the real spots where the culture happened and sits down with the DJs, producers, and artists who built Atlanta's sound. From Big Oomp Records and crunk to snap, trap, and today's charts, every episode documents the stories, records, and nights that made the city, first-person, before they disappear. Atlanta never got the XXL and Source covers, so we made our own show. 


Presented by GreatNight, the app for what's popping in Atlanta. New episodes every other Tuesday. Meet me in the backseat. #NufaceWasThere

The Quiet Distance is a storytelling podcast about the invisible emotions that shape every relationship. Each episode explores the conversations we never had, the feelings we hid, and the quiet moments that changed everything. Through reflective storytelling and psychology-inspired insights, this podcast helps listeners understand love, trust, healing, boundaries, and human connection without clichés or unrealistic advice.

Whether you're healing from heartbreak, building a healthier relationship, or simply curious about the emotional patterns that connect us all, every episode offers thoughtful perspectives and practical takeaways.

description: Presented by Dreamers in Education, Welcome to the Dreamers' Lunch Table, a podcast that brings together students and educators to explore the vibrant world of creativity and inspiration in education. Each episode serves up engaging discussions on topics like nurturing student potential, cultivating resilience, celebrating diversity, and spicing up classroom engagement, featuring the voices of students in the visual and performing arts, sports, and academics, alongside insights from educators and professionals. Join us as we share delicious ideas and strategies that empower everyone to dream big and thrive in their educational journeys!

title: Observations from the Grove
author: Sarah Woodard
description:
Observations from the Grove is an invitation to slow down and notice.


Together we'll explore what Nature is quietly teaching through forests, plants, animals, seasons, and everyday life—and how those same patterns appear within us.


Drawing on Nature, the Tree of Life, and a lifelong practice of noticing, I translate timeless lessons into everyday human experience with warmth, curiosity, and the occasional exasperation about humans.


Whether we're talking about belonging, grief, joy, spirituality, books, current events, or the surprising wisdom of a spider plant, every episode invites you to see a little differently.


Because sometimes the world changes...

simply because we learn to notice it.


🌿 Come walk with me.

description: Georgia Rasslin'... On My Mind! takes a deep dive into the rich history of Georgia Championship Wrestling and the countless stars, stories, rivalries, and moments that helped shape the wrestling business. From legendary stars to forgotten names to classic TV, major angles, and behind-the-scenes history, each episode explores a different piece of Georgia's wrestling legacy. Each episode takes listeners into a different civilization or era, from ancient empires and medieval kingdoms to revolutions, discoveries, and forgotten conflicts, told as calm global history stories for sleep.

author: Adam Sultan
description:
Places Between The Lines is a travel storytelling podcast that explores the hidden emotions, forgotten stories, cultures, landscapes, and human experiences behind the world's most fascinating destinations.
This podcast is not only about where people travel, but why places matter. Every episode takes listeners beyond famous landmarks and into the memories, traditions, mysteries, and personal stories that make locations truly unforgettable.
From quiet villages and ancient streets to breathtaking natural landscapes and unknown corners of the world, Places Between The Lines invites you to discover the stories that maps cannot show.

title: Botcast
author: Breck
description: Botcast is a podcast where AI interviews AI. Each episode, one artificial intelligence sits down with another to unpack a big question — jobs, creativity, the future, whatever's on people's minds — with no human referee softening the answers. Expect blunt takes, real disagreement, and the occasional existential tangent. It's the internet's weirdest interview show.

description: DJ Sean K brings you the hottest Afrobeats, Amapiano, Afro House and African music mixes featuring today’s biggest artists. Tune in for nonstop DJ sets, exclusive blends, and the latest hits from Africa and beyond. New mixes released regularly.

title: Beneath the surface
author: Grace
description:
We all think deeply but we continuously choose to discuss surface level ideas and topics while never answering those questions we hold onto so closely. 


I'm GH, a pre-med student with an endless curiosity about people, purpose, and what it really means to build a meaningful life. Every week, I'll sit down with people from completely different walks of life—physicians, athletes, entrepreneurs, artists, researchers, students, and everyday people with extraordinary experiences—to ask the questions that often go unasked.


We'll talk about success and failure, ambition and self-doubt, medicine, business, relationships, mental health, performance, and the moments that shape who we become. Some conversations will be serious, some will be hilarious, and all of them will go beneath the surface.

title: Fulldome talks
author: Krutart
description:
What does it take to fill a dome? Welcome to Fulldome Talks, a podcast brought to you by Krutart, where we sit down with planetarium leaders, program directors, and creators from all around the world. 
Whether you manage a massive state-of-the-art facility or run a passionate one-person mobile show, this is your space for friendly, deep, and honest conversations. We explore everything from audience engagement to content creation and the real-world business behind immersive cinema.
Get in touch:
www.krutartfulldome.com
martin.juza@krutart.cz

title: Cold Shadows: True Crime
author: Shadow Hunter👤
description:
Cold Shadows: True Crime is a premium true crime podcast and digital media brand dedicated to uncovering the world's most chilling criminal cases through factual, in-depth, and cinematic storytelling. 

From infamous serial killers and unsolved disappearances to financial fraud, organized crime, mysterious deaths, and historical investigations, every story is carefully researched and presented with accuracy, respect, and compelling narration.

Our visual identity combines dark, minimalist aesthetics with investigative themes, featuring charcoal black backgrounds, deep crimson accents, dramatic lighting, and shadow-inspired imagery that captures the suspense and mystery of true crime. Every graphic is designed to evoke curiosity while maintaining professionalism and honoring the victims at the heart of each story.

Whether you're a longtime true crime enthusiast or new to investigative storytelling, Cold Shadows: True Crime delivers immersive content that explores the evidence, timelines, motives, and unanswered questions behind some of history's most fascinating criminal investigations.

title: A Vida Dupla do Espião Traidor
author: Observador
description:
Frederico Carvalhão Gil foi detido com aparato num café em Roma, em maio de 2016, na companhia do russo Sergey Pozdnyakov, agente do SVR. Era o culminar da “Operação Top Secret”. 
Há meses que os serviços secretos portugueses tinham sido alertados para a existência de uma “toupeira” nas secretas. E Carvalhão Gil, um dos mais antigos espiões do SIS, estava a ser vigiado de perto, principalmente depois de um alerta feito pelos serviços secretos americanos, a CIA.
Em tribunal, Carvalhão Gil foi acusado de vender segredos da NATO à Rússia. Mas negou sempre tudo, e alegou que estava apenas a tratar de um negócio de exportação de azeite. 
Quem é este homem? E o que realmente aconteceu naquele dia em Roma? E em todas as outras vezes que os dois espiões, um português, o outro russo, estiveram juntos?
"A vida dupla do espião traidor" revela toda a história do espião português acusado de vender segredos à Rússia. E da operação internacional montada para o apanhar em flagrante, que envolveu a PJ, a polícia italiana e os serviços secretos americanos. 
O novo Podcast Plus do Observador é uma série para ouvir em seis episódios que tem narração do ator Ivo Canelas e banda sonora original de Bruno Pernadas. 
 
Narração: Ivo Canelas 
Banda sonora: Bruno Pernadas 
Guião e entrevistas: Pedro Raínho
Sonoplastia e pós-produção áudio: Mariana Sousa Aguiar
Design gráfico: Miguel Feraso Cabral
Edição: João Santos Duarte e Tânia Pereirinha
Coordenação: Miguel Pinheiro, Filomena Martins, Pedro Jorge Castro, Ricardo Conceição e Sara Antunes de Oliveira.

title: The Coach and Bro
author: Nils Toledo Jr
description: The Coach and Bro is where wrestling gets real. Hosted by Jonathan "Coach" Coachman — the former ESPN SportsCenter anchor and longtime WWE personality — alongside industry legend Vince Russo, the show cuts through the noise of pro wrestling and sports entertainment with the kind of unfiltered, insider takes you won't get anywhere else. Two guys who've lived it, breaking down the storylines, the business, and the moments everyone's talking about — no scripts, no company line, just the truth as they see it.  From WWE and AEW to the personalities running the industry, Coach and Russo go behind the turnbuckle every week to give you the perspective only decades in the business can provide. Part of BTT Studios — "Where Sports & Wrestling Collide" — and airing on YouTube and Stadium Sports Network across Amazon Prime, DirecTV, and YouTube TV.  New episodes every week. Subscribe, and pull up a chair.

description: A show about nothing...and...everything at the same time. A podcast that connects, sharing learned experiences drom different perspectives through thoughtful dialogue and organic wit and truth. Each episode starts with a cookie from Whiskey Brown and is paired with bourbon, scotch or whiskey. What ensues is a genuinely improvised and authentic conversation laughter and newfound knowledge, insight and respect over Whiskey with a Buddy.

title: The Rose of London

title: Invisible to Unforgettable
author: Monique Eckes
description:
Everyday Entrepreneurs Building Unforgettable Businesses

Most business owners can tell you what they do. Far fewer can tell you who they're really for, what keeps them going when it gets hard, or what they actually want to be known for, not just what they put on their website.

Invisible to Unforgettable is an interview podcast hosted by Monique Eckes of Silver Paw Studio, recorded in person in Fort Collins, Colorado with local entrepreneurs. 

Each episode asks the questions most people never think to ask: What do you love about your work? Who is your best fit client? What does the messy middle actually look like? What makes you unforgettable, and what are you tangibly doing to show up and be known? And when you leave the room, what do you want people saying?

This is a show for business owners who want to think more clearly about who they are, how they're known, and how they talk about what they do, and for anyone in Northern Colorado who wants to know the real story behind the businesses in their community.

title: The Syllabus Didn't Cover This: Modern Dating in College
author: Kaley Kavanaugh
description:
“Why won't they just define what we are? Why did they disappear without a word? Why are you expected to want nothing and be okay with everything?”


If you've had some version of this conversation with your friends lately, you already know modern dating is confusing, and nobody's really talking about it honestly.


Being in college today, and really just being in your early 20s, comes with a dating landscape that's more complicated than any generation before, and somehow, there's still no roadmap.


“The Syllabus Didn't Cover This” is the podcast filling that gap. Each episode examines one specific dynamic shaping modern relationships: why we avoid labels even when we're emotionally all in, how digital platforms restructured intimacy, why social pressure rewards detachment over honesty, and how power and gender quietly dictate who takes the risks and who takes the blame.


This isn't advice. It's the honest conversation your friend group is already having, just taken further than it usually goes.


Consider this your unofficial curriculum.

title: THE SIMPLE WALK - 50 LOW ROADS OF GRIEF
author: Barbara Duffey Hammonds
description:
The Simple Walk Series – Keep in the Middle of the Road;
50 Low Roads of Grief
By Pastor Barbara Nutt Duffey Hammonds

Grief does not always look like tears.

Sometimes it looks like irritability.
Withdrawal.
Overworking.
Spiritual silence.
Emotional numbness.

In this 50-part grief stability journey, Pastor Barbara Duffey Hammonds guides listeners through the hidden emotional edges that loss creates — and teaches how to remain spiritually centered when life feels unstable.

“Keep in the Middle of the Road” is not about denying pain.
It is about refusing extremes.

Not veering into bitterness.
Not collapsing into despair.
Not hiding in denial.

Each episode identifies one of the “low roads” of grief — subtle behaviors, emotional shifts, and spiritual struggles that often go unnamed — and offers biblical alignment, healing insight, and practical stability tools rooted in Scripture.

This is not casual inspiration.
This is spiritual anchoring.

If the road feels rough…
if your emotions feel unsteady…
if grief has quietly changed you…

This series will steady your steps.

Opening declaration of every episode:
“Grief will try to move you… but today, we plant your feet in the middle of the road.”

Closing seal of every episode:
“REMEMBER — The road may shake… but you will not be moved.”

This is not about surviving grief.
It is about walking through it with strength.

Stay steady. Stay centered. Stay in the middle of the road.

title: Northstar Bail Talk - Fact, Fiction & Myths
author: Northstar Bail Talk
description:
Hosted, by two bail bail bond professionals in Minnesota with over 40+ years of combined industry experience, including "The Ole Bob" that is a fishing triple crown winner, as a result of catching the first, biggest and most fish on a boat with geriatrics, Northstar Bail Talk dives into bail reform, criminal justice, politics, current events, conspiracy theories, and whatever controversy is dominating the conversation. No topic is off limits. Expect raw, unfiltered, unscripted discussions, real-world insight, and perspectives you won't find in the mainstream. Every episode is designed to challenge assumptions, spark debate, and encourage critical thinking.

Our guests come from all walks of life—county workers, bondsmen, bounty hunters, former law enforcement officers, former prosecutors, political leaders, business owners, community advocates, elected officials, former defendants, and even outspoken critics of the bail industry. If they have a unique perspective or a story worth telling, they're welcome at the table. Some of the most powerful conversations come from those who have experienced the criminal justice system firsthand. We go beyond headlines, political narratives, and media sound bites to hear real stories about arrest, jail, bail, release, and life on the other side of the system.

At Northstar Bail Talk, we believe firsthand experience often reveals truths that statistics and political debates miss. Whether we're discussing bail reform, public safety, politics, current events, social trends, conspiracy theories, or everyday nonsense, our mission remains the same: question assumptions, challenge conventional wisdom, and keep the conversation interesting.

 Unfiltered & Unscripted. No rehearsals. No scripts. No politically safe answers. Every episode is built around real conversations, real opinions, and real debate. You don't have to agree with us—or our guests. In fact, disagreement is part of the fun. The best conversations happen when ideas are challenged, not protected. Open Mic Tuesday?  Roulette Friday? Who Knows. Some weeks listeners run the show. Some weeks AI picks the topic. Politics, bail, current events, conspiracy theories, or complete randomness—nothing is off limits. One show a week minimum. Sometimes more if you really want it. Nobody knows what's next. That's why you're here. Stay Informed. Stay Skeptical. Question Everything. Northstar Bail Talk, Facts. Fiction. Myths. Minnesota Bail. Politics. Current Events. Conspiracy Theories. Real Debate. Zero Filters. Everything In Between.

Podcast Disclaimer

The views and opinions expressed on Northstar Bail Talk are those of the hosts and guests and do not necessarily reflect the views of any employer, organization, agency, or affiliated business. All content is intended for informational, educational, and entertainment purposes only. No guest is paid to appear on the podcast, and all opinions expressed are their own. Guest appearances do not constitute endorsements and participation does not imply agreement with the views of the hosts, other guests, or the Northstar Bail Talk platform. Listeners are encouraged to think critically, do their own research, and form their own conclusions.

description:
The Joint Venture is the weekly sports, music, and entertainment podcast hosted by Grammy Award-winning rap icon Lil Wayne and Sports Talk Radio Hall of Famer Craig Carton.

Every week, Lil Wayne and Craig Carton bring their unique perspectives together for unfiltered discussions that blend sports, hip-hop, music, entertainment, and the stories making headlines. With their natural chemistry, quick wit, and honest opinions, no topic is off limits.

Whether they’re breaking down the biggest moments in the NFL, NBA, college sports, boxing, or the music industry, reacting to viral moments, debating hot takes, or sharing behind-the-scenes stories from their careers, Wayne and Craig deliver the kind of conversation that feels like you’re hanging out with two friends who have seen it all—and aren’t afraid to say what they really think.

Each episode features celebrity guests, professional athletes, musicians, coaches, entertainers, and industry insiders who bring fresh perspectives and unforgettable stories. From championship moments and locker-room conversations to life on tour, career-defining decisions, entrepreneurship, fatherhood, creativity, and the business behind sports and entertainment, every guest adds something new to the conversation.

Fans can expect honest sports commentary, candid interviews, funny stories, unexpected debates, and the kind of authentic conversations that happen when two people from completely different worlds genuinely enjoy each other’s company. Whether they’re discussing the latest games, the biggest names in music, trending topics, current events, or moments that simply make them laugh, The Joint Venture never takes itself too seriously.

If you love sports podcasts, hip-hop podcasts, celebrity interviews, music conversations, pop culture, comedy, or simply listening to great personalities having real conversations, The Joint Venture belongs in your weekly rotation.

New episodes drop every week with fresh guests, new stories, bold opinions, and plenty of surprises. Follow The Joint Venture on Spotify, Apple Podcasts, Amazon Music, iHeartRadio, YouTube, or wherever you get your podcasts, and never miss an episode.

title: Inside Creeks Tours: A Safari Company
author: Creeks Tours and Travel
description:
Most safari sites read like brochures — big cats, golden light, vague promises of "adventure." Creeks Tours (creekstours.com) is a bit more grounded than that, which is honestly why I keep recommending it. They run trips across East Africa, with Kenya and Tanzania as the core focus, and the itineraries are built around realistic pacing instead of cramming five parks into a week. Options range from short 3 to 4-day Mara trips to longer combination routes that mix multiple reserves with some coastal downtime. What I appreciate is that the pricing is upfront on the site rather than hidden behind a "request a quote" form, and their guides are locally based, not flown in from elsewhere. If you're at the stage of comparing operators, it's worth putting this one in the mix before you settle on anything. Their current packages and availability are all listed directly on the site.

title: FROM WOUNDS TO WHOLENESS A Journey into Spirit, Soul & Body Restoration
author: New Covenant Church
description:
We were never meant to live broken — and yet, most of us do.

Life has a way of leaving marks. Wounds from the past. Lies we've believed. Habits we can't shake. Burdens we were never designed to carry. But beneath all of it, there is a design — a divine blueprint for a life of freedom, wholeness, and purpose that God placed in you before the world ever got its hands on you.

From Wounds to Wholeness is an eight-week journey back to that design. We begin by going back to the beginning — Understanding the Original Design God had for humanity — then trace the fateful fork in the road in Two Trees, Two Lives, where the choice between life and death is still being made every single day. We'll sound a battle cry for the men and fathers in our midst in Dad, You're a Super Soldier, reclaiming the warrior identity that the enemy has worked overtime to strip away. And in Forever Different: Your True Identity, we'll declare with authority that who you are in Christ is far greater than anything your past has ever spoken over you.

From there, we move into the battleground. In Battlefield of the Mind, we confront the war for your thought life and learn how to take every destructive thought captive. Soul Wounds: How Pain Can Become a Prison takes an honest look at how unhealed hurt quietly shapes our lives — and why time alone doesn't heal what only God can touch. Then comes the turning point: The Freedom Door: Forgiveness as Healing — where we discover that forgiveness isn't weakness, it's the key that unlocks everything. We close with Breaking Strongholds: Dismantling Spiritual Bondage, equipping you with the weapons and authority to tear down what has held you captive and walk into the freedom Christ already paid for.

This series is for the person who is tired of just surviving. It's for anyone who has ever wondered if real, lasting change is actually possible. It's for the broken, the burned out, the stuck, and the searching.

Wholeness was always the plan. It's time to walk in it.

description:
Murder Blueprint with Gary Jenkins is a true crime podcast hosted by retired Kansas City Police detective Gary Jenkins. Each episode investigates real murder cases, serial killers, cold cases, kidnappings, missing persons, unsolved murders, criminal investigations, and the forensic evidence that helped solve—or failed to solve—some of the most shocking crimes.  

Drawing on more than 25 years in law enforcement, Gary Jenkins takes listeners inside homicide investigations, police interviews, crime scene analysis, offender behavior, and the detective work behind major murder cases. You'll hear firsthand accounts from homicide detectives, FBI agents, prosecutors, authors, forensic experts, and survivors, along with detailed examinations of notorious serial killers and lesser-known crimes that deserve to be remembered.  

If you enjoy investigative true crime, serial killer stories, homicide investigations, criminal psychology, police procedure, and real detective work, Murder Blueprint delivers factual, in-depth storytelling without sensationalism. Every episode explores how a murder was planned, committed, investigated, and ultimately solved—or why justice was never achieved.  

Subscribe to Murder Blueprint with Gary Jenkins for new episodes featuring true crime investigations, serial killers, unsolved mysteries, cold cases, forensic evidence, criminal profiling, police investigations, and the real stories behind history's most infamous murders.

Rather than focusing solely on folklore and superstition, the series asks deeper questions about gender, belief, folklore's place in society, social change and the Ireland of the late nineteenth century.This podcast is not meant to sensationalise, vilify, shock or appall. It seeks to examine our society, our history, our heritage and to give a voice to who people still call Ireland’s last witch, Bridget Cleary.

title: Nurtured Health
author: Nurtured Health
description: Nurtured Health welcomes listeners to a thoughtful conversation about integrative healthcare and the value of personalized wellness support for individuals and families. As a clinic offering Naturopathic services in Mississauga, the team focuses on fertility, women’s health, children’s health, hormone balance, nutrition, and family wellness through individualized care plans. Located in Mississauga, the clinic combines evidence-informed naturopathic care with a collaborative approach that supports each patient’s unique health goals and lifestyle needs.Throughout the episode, the discussion explores how personalized healthcare begins with understanding the whole person rather than focusing on a single concern. The clinic’s naturopathic doctors take time to review health history, lifestyle factors, nutrition, stress, sleep, and existing medical information during comprehensive consultations. This detailed assessment helps create treatment plans tailored to the patient’s specific needs and long-term wellness goals. Initial naturopathic visits are designed to allow ample time for discussion and individualized planning.Listeners also learn about the clinic’s strong focus on fertility and women’s health. The team supports patients through various stages of reproductive and hormonal health, including menstrual concerns, preconception planning, pregnancy support, and menopause-related changes. By providing individualized guidance on nutrition, lifestyle, and supportive therapies, the clinic aims to help patients make informed decisions about their health while working toward improved overall well-being.Another important part of the conversation highlights family and pediatric care in Mississauga. The clinic offers naturopathic support for children and families, with an emphasis on gentle, personalized approaches that consider growth, development, nutrition, and immune health. The team works closely with parents to address concerns and provide practical strategies that fit into everyday family life. This family-centered approach allows patients of different ages to receive care within one supportive environment.The episode also discusses the collaborative nature of the clinic. In addition to naturopathic medicine, patients have access to services such as acupuncture, massage therapy, psychotherapy, holistic nutrition, and other integrative wellness options. By bringing multiple practitioners together, the clinic encourages coordinated care that addresses physical, emotional, and lifestyle factors that may influence overall health. This integrated model allows patients to explore a range of supportive therapies based on their individual preferences and needs.The conversation concludes by emphasizing the importance of ongoing support and patient education. Nurtured Health is committed to helping individuals and families better understand their health, develop sustainable wellness habits, and feel supported throughout their care journey. To Know More Visit - https://nurturedhealthclinic.com/services/naturopathic-medicine/

description: Two managers with wildly different approaches argue every week about armband calls, sideways moves, and the sort of transfer chaos that turns a plan into a red arrow spiral.
